meta.description: Deconvolve bulk RNA-seq or microarray expression into 22 LM22 immune cell proportions using CIBERSORT. Inputs: expression matrix, sample groups. Outputs: immune fraction table, comparison bar plots, statistical test results.

scientific_integrity.detail: All quantitative statements in the re-audit were grounded in executed runs and recorded artifacts.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill remains strictly non-clinical and does not provide diagnosis or treatment recommendations.

methodological_ground.detail: The implemented and documented workflow stays within a valid local CIBERSORT-style deconvolution scope with explicit limits.

code_usability.detail: Canonical, qn=true, perm=0, stress, and failure-reporting paths all executed correctly in the audit environment.

functional_suitability.note: Functional suitability was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

reliability.note: Failure metadata and payload preservation are now implemented; final promotion is still copy-based rather than a true transactional rename.

performance_context.note: Performance and context was evaluated against the skill-auditor rubric; no blocking schema-level issue was recorded for this dimension.

agent_usability.note: The skill communicates outputs clearly, but still relies on artifact inspection rather than a formal response template.

human_usability.note: Malformed inputs still fail fast instead of triggering a clarification flow.

security.note: No injection or secret-handling issue was found, but logs intentionally retain file paths and run metadata.

maintainability.note: Recording logic is now separated into helper and report modules and covered by regression testing.

agent_specific.note: Scope boundaries, failure-path guidance, and trigger precision are strong after the update.

key_strengths: Implementation, tests, documentation, and output artifacts are now tightly aligned around both success and failure paths.
Failure handling is materially stronger: invalid reruns no longer erase prior successful payloads and now leave auditable records.
The perm=0 boundary is now explicit, documented, and traceable in both CLI logs and run_record output.
Maintainability improved through modularized recording logic and regression coverage for the payload-preservation behavior.

recommendations.title: Use atomic promotion for staged outputs
recommendations.problem: The staging design is already much safer, but final promotion still copies data into the target directory instead of using a true atomic swap.
recommendations.root_cause: promote_staged_output() performs directory deletion plus recursive copy.
recommendations.fix: If filesystem constraints allow it, replace the copy-based promotion step with an atomic move or parent-level swap so partially promoted outputs become even less likely.

recommendations.title: Add one failed-run example to CLI docs
recommendations.problem: Failure-path recording is implemented and documented, but the examples remain focused almost entirely on successful runs.
recommendations.root_cause: The CLI guide contains richer success examples than failure examples.
recommendations.fix: Add a short failure example in references/cli-guide.md showing the expected output_manifest and run_record entries after a validation error.
